what_it_does: Vitest test suite for the clone:// fleet-recall path. It seeds a temporary SQLite index with two clone:// reflection units and two claude conversation exchanges, then verifies that the generic sources IN-list filter scopes results to clone, to claude, to both, to empty, and composes with session_ids. It also tests the clone capture helpers: address parsing, deterministic unit id generation, record-to-exchange stamping, and the three-way parseCloneGet behavior (populated reflection, empty/skip, and fail-loud error).
observations: Uses TEXT search mode so the suite stays self-contained and avoids live embeddings.
Inserts each clone unit both as an exchange and as a unit via registerUnitType/insertUnit.
All four seeded records share the literal marker 'shared-clone-marker' so the only scoping variable is the source filter.
The parser tests explicitly check that a mid-body em-dash does not truncate the reflection block.
Empty source array [] is required to return [] rather than falling through to the whole corpus.
recall() results are expected to carry provenance equal to the canonical clone://<sid>/<cut> session id.
standouts: The three-way parseCloneGet contract: populated / empty-skip / fail-loud.
The insistence that sources:['clone'] works through the GENERIC filter with no special-casing.
Deterministic idempotence: cloneUnitId is derived from the canonical address.
Fail-loud behavior on missing or error-shaped clone-get output.
conventions: Follows the Vitest describe/it/expect pattern with beforeEach/afterEach temp directory cleanup.
Uses environment variable TEST_DB_PATH to redirect the database into a temp dir.
Helper functions cloneExchange and claudeExchange build seed ConversationExchange objects.
Heavy inline comments state the intent of each test block.
concerns: The filler array new Array(2560).fill(1) is an unexplained magic shape for the embedding column.
Parser tests rely on exact whitespace and punctuation in the raw clone-get output, making them brittle if the CLI formatting changes.
The file asserts model='—' is normalized to '' without showing the normalization logic.
No automated coverage for the live-embed end-to-end path; it is explicitly left as manual/scripted verification.
notes: A self-contained behavioral contract test: it says clone:// must scope like any other source, and clone capture must parse deterministically and fail loud.
questions: What exact rule does parseCloneGet use to treat 'reflection: —' as empty while preserving mid-body em-dashes?
Why is each clone record inserted both as an exchange and as a unit?
What does the 2560-element filler array represent in the embedding schema?
How does the generic source filter interact with vector-rank vs text-rank ordering in other modes?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it verifies generic source scoping and clone capture/parser behavior, and the tests do exactly that.
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish clone:// reflection records as a first-class, address-stable recall source that behaves identically to board/claude sources in the filter layer, while keeping capture parsing robust and explicit about missing or malformed data.
novelty: The clone:// URI addressing scheme with source SID and cut, plus a three-way parser contract that distinguishes populated reflection, intentional empty-skip, and error states.
contribution: A concrete, runnable behavioral specification for how clone:// units must participate in source-scoped recall and how raw clone-get output must be transformed into recall-queryable exchanges.
summary_for_embedding: A Vitest test file that verifies the generic source-scope filter isolates clone:// reflection units and that clone capture produces deterministic, canonical, fail-loud parsed exchanges.
intention_for_embedding: To prove clone:// records integrate into recall as a generic source with stable addressing and a robust three-way parser.

symbols.does: Builds a ConversationExchange for a clone:// record with harness='clone' and sessionId set to the canonical address.
symbols.description: Helper that constructs a clone reflection exchange for a given cut, era label, and body, stamping the project, harness, and canonical clone://<sid>/<cut> archive path/session id so it can be inserted into the recall index.

symbols.does: Builds a ConversationExchange for a claude conversation source with harness='claude'.
symbols.description: Helper that constructs a plain conversation exchange for a given id, session id, and text, used as a non-clone control to verify that the generic source filter scopes clone and claude records apart.
